learning_objectives: Compare opioid classes (full mu, partial mu, kappa) and identify species-specific differences in receptor response and practical administration routes
Explain tramadol M1 metabolite, CYP2D6 hepatic conversion, and why dogs often have limited opioid-pathway analgesic response
Identify anti-NGF monoclonal antibodies (frunevetmab/Solensia for cats; bedinvetmab/Librela for dogs) and their mechanism and renal-safety profile
Apply gabapentin dose adjustment for CKD cats: half-life extends from ~4 hours to 13-20 hours in IRIS Stage 3-4
Recognize high-risk prescribing combinations: NSAID + corticosteroid, bisphosphonate + active dental disease
Describe the zoledronate protocol: 0.1-0.5 mg/kg IV over exactly 15 minutes in normal saline only, maximum 4 mg
Construct opioid-sparing perioperative protocols combining NSAID, gabapentinoid, NMDA antagonist, and locoregional technique
high_priority_objectives: Apply gabapentin dose adjustment for CKD cats and recognize NSAIDs as contraindicated in IRIS Stage 3-4
Recognize high-risk prescribing combinations and respond appropriately
mastery_threshold_note: Fellows must demonstrate specific drug doses, routes, and contraindication reasoning. Generic drug lists without doses or mechanisms indicate insufficient mastery.
